← All Digests Thursday, March 5, 2026

The AI Execution Gap: 91% Adoption, 6% Deployed, 41% Can Prove ROI

Today's top stories in AI + Product Marketing

1

Only 6% of Marketers Have Fully Deployed AI — Despite 80% Urgency

New Supermetrics data across US, UK, Germany, Australia, and Singapore shows the AI execution gap is real. While 89% of adoption pressure comes from the C-suite, the bottleneck isn't motivation — it's data access. 52% of marketers lack control over their own data strategy, and 37% cite poor system integration with analytics tools.

Read on eMarketer →
2

MarTech: 91% Using AI But Only 41% Can Prove ROI

Jasper's State of AI in Marketing Report shows nearly everyone has AI in their stack, but fewer can demonstrate value — down from nearly half last year. The culprit: 75% of companies don't have a real AI roadmap. High-maturity organizations are 2x more likely to achieve solid ROI because they map AI use cases to actual business results, not just activity boxes.

Read on MarTech →
3

The Next Frontier: Agentic Conversation Intelligence

Tech AI Magazine outlines how multi-agent AI systems are turning customer conversations into real-time operational signals. Instead of just recording and summarizing calls, coordinated AI agents can detect intent, score opportunities, route interactions, and trigger CRM/CDP workflows in near real-time. Every call becomes a streaming data signal, not a post-call report.

Read on Tech AI Magazine →
4

FactSet Creates Chief AI Officer Role

Enterprise financial data provider FactSet appointed Kate Stepp as Chief AI Officer to lead AI services across products and client solutions. The move signals how serious enterprise software companies are getting about dedicated AI leadership — not just tacking AI onto existing tech roles.

Read on GlobeNewswire →
5

Claude Code Builds Full Marketing Campaign in 50 Minutes

Geeky Gadgets shows how Claude Code can create a complete inbound marketing campaign — landing page, lead magnet, and email sequence — in under an hour. The tool's reusable "skills" system lets marketers save task-specific instructions for repeatable workflows. For PMMs watching AI eat their workflows: this is what it looks like.

Read on Geeky Gadgets →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The eMarketer piece on the AI execution gap. The 6% deployment stat is the headline, but the real insight is that 52% of marketers don't control their own data strategy. You can't AI your way out of a data governance problem. The companies closing the gap will be the ones who fix the plumbing first, then turn on the AI.
